_inspectorSnowReport.Report:
3/13 4:25 PM Update: Mountain Operations for tomorrow remain very unlikely due to continued power outages, Highway 410 hazards and closures, and the severe weather. We will update the mountain report as soon as we know more, but do not expect any updates until after 10am. Please do not attempt to drive 410 until WSDOT officially reopens the road. 
  • All day and night ski tickets for today 3/13 will be automatically refunded. No email required. If we do not open tomorrow 3/14, all day and night ski tickets will also be automatically refunded.
  • Parking Reservation cancellations will NOT be required for tomorrow, 3/14. Our no show policy will not be enforced. 
  • All Crystal Express Shuttle departures are cancelled for tomorrow.
  • All Lessons Programs and Ski School Operations are cancelled for tomorrow, and will be rescheduled.
The Boulevard is now back open to all traffic. Per WSDOT, both lanes of Highway 410 are still closed at Mud Mountain with no timeframe for reopening. Refer to the WSDOT site for the most up to date information. 

A message to the Crystal Community
Good afternoon Crystal Skiers and Riders,
 
There's no other way to say it: today was a tough one. While we're overjoyed to see the return of much‑needed snowfall, this amount of snow and severe weather brings exceptional challenges. We are deeply grateful for you, the Crystal Community, for your support and understanding, and for our Crystal Crew, whose tireless efforts have carried us through this wild week of weather.
 
Today, Crystal Mountain teams were hard at work in the early morning hours to prepare the resort for opening. Around mid‑morning, the Washington State Department of Transportation officially closed Highway 410 at Mud Mountain Road, and due to the weather‑related nature of this closure, there was no estimated time for reopening.
 
While we were hopeful the road could reopen later as it did yesterday afternoon, unfortunately today was different. There were many reports of large downed trees and powerlines, and continued wet and heavy snowfall all the way to Enumclaw. It became clear the road would not reopen anytime soon when State police began to turn cars around.
 
With ongoing power outages on the mountain limiting lift operations, no access for emergency vehicles, and dangerous road conditions on the boulevard, we made the decision to close the mountain for the day for everyone's safety.
 
The snow is still coming down heavily, With safety in mind, we know Mother Nature always has the final say. Continue to refer WSDOT for the status of Highway 410, and refer to the Mountain Report for all mountain operations info going into tomorrow. 
 
Thank you, and stay safe out there today!
–Crystal Mountain
______________________________

Notes for today:
  1.  This is the last weekend of night skiing coming up March 12th-15th! Take advantage of all the new snow on your schedule. Enjoy our Coca Cola Ride and Dine deal for just $75. Included is a night skiing ticket (1pm-7pm), dinner voucher and a Coke. 
  2. $199 Spring 3-Day Passes are on sale now! Ski for just $67 a day, any day of the week, for the rest of the season!
  3. $62 Day tickets continue every Tuesday for the remainder of the season!
  4. Looking to save time and make the most of the day? Consider grabbing a Daily Reserve Upgrade! Available on weekends and holidays, this upgrade grants access to priority lift lines, getting you on the lift faster and allowing you to complete more laps. 
  5. Did you know it just takes 5 Days to be entered to win a 26/27 Ikon Pass? Track your days on the Ikon App and register for RIDE TO WIN
  6. Paid reservations can only be cancelled by emailing SP+ at  CrystalMtnParking@spplus.com with Reservation #, First Name, and Last Name. While you may not receive an immediate reply, the cancellation will be processed. The Crystal Mountain Contact Center cannot process these cancellations.
  7. In case of on-mountain emergency: Call 360-663-3064 or 911

Mountain Weather 3/12/2026 5:30pm 

Forecaster: Mike Haft  
  
Synopsis:   
Crystal is getting the beans right now and the pounding will continue into the evening. Seeya when we can open. 
  
*Daily Summaries: 12hr period representing 4am-4pm or 4pm-4am data
*Freezing Elevation (ft): Approximate elevation above sea level where the air temperature reaches 32 degree F

Uphill Travel Update: See the above banner for the current status. Please visit guest services to get your 25/26 uphill travel card. This must be renewed free of charge for the season. Uphill travel may be prohibited at any time as mountain operations teams conduct avalanche mitigation. Refer to the "No Uphill Travel" lights around the base area and the Mountain Report page indicating the status of the designated route. These lights are located by the Gondola trail map, at the bottom of Chinook, at the bottom of Discovery, and at the top of the Quicksilver lift shack. For more information about uphill travel, please visit the uphill travel page. 

25/26 WINTER PROGRAMS, CLINICS, AND NECESSITIES

  • Ski and Ride School: Explore our programs and find the right fit for you.
  • AIARE Course & Certification: Ski the backcountry with expert knowledge and certification

_inspectorSnowReport.Alert:
Uphill Travel Status: NOT PERMITTED
3/13 9:45 AM Update: Due to severe weather on the mountain, ongoing power outages, employee access, and WSDOT road closure on Hwy 410, Crystal Mountain will not be operating any lifts today.
